name: Chip
description: Informational chip. Square-cornered metadata label. Passive (or dismissable via trailing ×). No leading icon. Two appearances: `default` paints a translucent black/white overlay (light → `ref.palette.black.200`, dark → `ref.palette.white.200`) so the tag adopts whatever surface sits behind it; `accent` paints a tonal pale-primary container (`sys.color.primaryContainer`) with primary label for tags that need to pop against the surface.

trailingIcon.description: Dismiss glyph (×); when present, chip becomes interactive.
Dismiss / opt-out glyph after the label.

default.label: sys.color.onSurface
default.note: Background is a translucent black/white overlay (`black.200` in light mode, `white.200` in dark) so the tag harmonises with whatever surface sits behind it — body, raised card, BottomSheet — instead of pinning to a fixed neutral step. Driven by CSS class (not inline `--chip-bg`) so the dark-theme override actually applies; inline emission from JSX would override the `[data-theme="dark"]` rule.

accent.label: sys.color.primary
accent.note: Tonal accent: pale primary container background with primary label. Sys-color tokens are theme-aware, so no separate dark binding is needed — the resolved tokens pick the right values per theme. Use for tags that should pop against the surface (e.g. Popular Tags in compose, highlighted hashtags) where the default overlay is too quiet.

focusIndicator.description: Keyboard-focus visual — an accessibility indicator, not a lifecycle state. Composes over whichever lifecycle state the chip is in. Applies only when the chip is interactive (a dismiss trailingIcon is present). The `states.focused` block above is kept for JSX runtime consumers; this block is the parallel external-reader contract.
focusIndicator.compositionReason: Action affordance with breathing room around it; the 3px outward extent is reserved by the surrounding layout.
focusIndicator.trigger: :focus-visible (keyboard / programmatic focus, never plain mouse click)
